data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-1bca5898">
    <title>availability: recent usage on drilldown via extended agentSessions filters; expose AgentSession.provider + WorkerExecutionResult.Tier</title>
    <description>
Recent usage panel on per-provider drilldown. Extend agentSessions filter to support provider scope. Expose AgentSession.provider and WorkerExecutionResult.Tier in GraphQL schema. Forward-only — no historical backfill.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. agentSessions resolver supports provider filter. 2. AgentSession.provider field exposed. 3. WorkerExecutionResult.Tier exposed. 4. Drilldown shows recent usage rows.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:9, area:server, kind:feature</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-12e97cb9" base-rev="2078597b6680b5980d168341cece2d36234a6230" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T090347-82d93a92/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `6e84deba` with subject `feat: extend agent sessions drilldown with provider and tier [ddx-1bca5898]`.

What changed:
- Provider filtering now flows into session-index reads in [cli/internal/agent/session_index.go:61](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/agent/session_index.go#L61) and is threaded through GraphQL in [cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:634](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go#L634) plus [cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent.go:44](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent.go#L44).
- `AgentSession.provider` and `WorkerExecutionResult.tier` are exposed in the schema at [cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:623](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql#L623), [cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:683](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql#L683), and [cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2505](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql#L2505).
- The recent-usage panel was added to the provider drilldown page in [cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/[name]/+page.svelte:24](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/[name]/+page.svelte#L24) and rendered at [cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/[name]/+page.svelte:263](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/[name]/+page.svelte#L263).
- Coverage was added in [cli/internal/server/state_graphql_provider_test.go:10](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/state_graphql_provider_test.go#L10) and the drilldown e2e at [cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/agent-endpoints.e2e.ts:324](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-1bca5898-20260505T090347-82d93a92/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/agent-endpoints.e2e.ts#L324).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...`
- `bunx playwright test 'agent-endpoints\\.e2e\\.ts$'`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

The pre-commit hook uses `-race`, so it needed `CGO_ENABLED=1` in this environment.
